Kennedy assassination governor of Texas seriously injured Kennedy assassination governor of Texas seriously injured Kennedy assassination governor of Texas seriously injured At the time of President John F. Kennedy's assassination on November 22, 1963, the governor of Texas was John Bowden Connally. Connally was seriously injured during the assassination. He was riding in the presidential limousine, seated in front of Kennedy, and was hit by the same bullet that struck Kennedy, suffering multiple wounds.
